rankings.title: Snap’s GPU-Accelerated Secret to Processing 10 Petabytes a Day | NVIDIA AI Podcast Ep. 298
summary_bullets: Processes 10+ petabytes daily for experiments.
Migrated Spark jobs to GPUs with zero code changes.
Cut costs 76%, memory 80%, disk spill 120 TB.
Used idle inference GPUs during low-traffic hours.
Built fallback system from GPUs to CPUs to Dataproc.

rankings.title: Inside AI Tokenomics: How to Profitably Turn Tokens Into Business Value | NVIDIA AI Podcast Ep. 299
summary_bullets: Token value depends on intelligence and speed.
Demand forecasting uses users, requests, tokens per request.
Cost per token beats input metrics like GPU cost.
Blackwell delivers 50x more tokens per watt than Hopper.
Extreme co-design spans compute, memory, networking, software.

rankings.title: Inside Instacart's AI-Powered Smart Shopping Cart | NVIDIA AI Podcast Ep. 302
summary_bullets: Caper Cart uses NVIDIA Jetson for edge AI.

AI recommendations drive double-digit sales lift.
Vision: unified online/in-store shopping in 5-10 years.

rankings.title: Why Cost Per Token Is the Only Metric You Need for AI TCO
summary_bullets: Cost per token measures AI factory output.
Power efficiency directly affects token cost.
Data center overhead can double energy waste.
Token demand grows faster than compute supply.
GPU-hour metric ignores inefficiencies.

rankings.title: One Brain, Any Robot: Skild AI's Skild Brain Explained | NVIDIA AI Podcast Ep. 295
summary_bullets: Robotics is a data problem with scarce data.
Building a universal brain for any robot form factor.

Pre-training on video/simulation, post-training on real data.

rankings.title: Harrison Chase of LangChain on Deep Agents, LangSmith, and Earning Trust | NVIDIA AI Podcast Ep. 297
summary_bullets: Deep Agents is a general-purpose agent harness.

Future agents will be proactive, with memory and identity.

rankings.title: How AI Will Change Quantum Computing | NVIDIA AI Podcast Ep. 294
summary_bullets: Quantum computing uses qubits, not bits.
